title: Verses On The Upright Judge, Who Condemned The Drapier's Printer
content:
The church I hate, and have good reason,
    For there my grandsire cut his weasand:
    He cut his weasand at the altar;
    I keep my gullet for the halter.
meaning:
"Verses On The Upright Judge, Who Condemned The Drapier's Printer" is a quintessential example of Jonathan Swift's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
